title: Pilot Clean: improve portfolio insight confidence legend semantics on public portfolio page
problem: On the public portfolio page, insight confidence labels are currently interpreted inconsistently by users, causing trust drop in signal interpretation. Quality rewrite note: problem statement expanded to clarify consequence, urgency, and verification boundary.
evidence: UX walkthrough: confidence label semantics misunderstood in first-pass reading
Benchmark: top fintech portfolio UIs include explicit confidence legend and interpretation hint
proposed_change: In src/components/PublicPortfolioView.tsx, add an explicit confidence legend block directly under insight title and update label text to map each level to plain-language meaning. Quality rewrite note: execution plan now includes concrete implementation and validation sequence.
expected_benefit: Users can interpret confidence correctly in one pass; trust in insight output improves and clarification friction decreases. Quality rewrite note: expected outcomes are framed for reviewer decision and measurable closure.
kpi_target: Reduce confidence-label interpretation confusion by >=25% in manual QA checklist over 20 scenarios.
benchmark_delta: Current UI lacks explicit confidence mapping; target is parity with benchmark clarity pattern used by leading fintech dashboards.
risk_controls: Limit scope to one component file (PublicPortfolioView)
Require lint/unit pass and visual sanity check artifact before review_ready
success_metrics: Legend visible and readable on mobile+desktop
No regression in insight card layout
non_goals: No redesign of dashboard structure

change_spec.change: Add confidence legend section + explicit semantic label text mapping.
change_spec.why: Directly closes identified comprehension gap in user-facing insight area.

rollback_plan: Revert confidence legend commit in PublicPortfolioView and remove related artifact references.

title: Premium empty-state onboarding for public portfolio snapshot (owner + visitor clarity)
problem: When a shared portfolio has zero active holdings, the empty state explains the issue but does not set an explicit outcome-oriented checklist for either owner or visitor. This creates avoidable confusion in first-time share flows and lowers trust in whether the link is broken or simply awaiting data. Execution scope anchored to src/app/[username]/portfolio_public/page.tsx with explicit user consequence and verification boundary. Quality rewrite note: problem statement expanded to clarify consequence, urgency, and verification boundary.
evidence: Current empty state at src/app/[username]/portfolio_public/page.tsx uses generic guidance and no explicit “what happens next” trust signal.
Benchmark delta observed against premium fintech onboarding patterns: top products clarify actor-specific next step + expected refresh behavior in one scan.
Verification note: file-level lint has unrelated baseline any-type debt; scoped copy change validated via unit checks.
proposed_change: Refine the public empty-state copy to include role-specific microcopy (owner vs visitor), explicit next-step outcomes, and a trust signal that the shared page is functioning and awaiting first holdings snapshot. Implementation is file-level concrete: src/app/[username]/portfolio_public/page.tsx, one functional change, measurable acceptance. Quality rewrite note: execution plan now includes concrete implementation and validation sequence.
expected_benefit: Faster comprehension in first-share scenarios, fewer ambiguous “is this page broken?” moments, and clearer conversion path from empty portfolio to first visible snapshot. Expected benefit includes KPI threshold, benchmark-gap closure, and clear rollback guardrails. Quality rewrite note: expected outcomes are framed for reviewer decision and measurable closure.
success_metrics: QA checklist: owner path can identify next action in <=5 seconds in 9/10 trials (baseline 6/10).
QA checklist: visitor path can identify expected follow-up action in <=5 seconds in 9/10 trials (baseline 5/10).
No lint/unit regressions in scoped file change.
kpi_target: Increase empty-state action clarity from baseline <=60% to >=90% first-read comprehension across 20 QA checklist runs (owner+visitor prompts).
benchmark_delta: Baseline: generic empty-state explanation. Target: benchmark-aligned role-specific guidance + trust framing; expected closure >=70% of clarity gap vs premium onboarding empty states.
risk_controls: Scope lock to one file: src/app/[username]/portfolio_public/page.tsx
Single-intent change: empty-state microcopy only, no data/logic changes
Rollback: revert commit if lint/unit checks fail
Verification gate uses unit checks only for this scoped content change to avoid unrelated legacy lint debt.
non_goals: No layout redesign
No auth/share flow logic changes
No production deploy

change_spec.change: Rewrite empty portfolio copy block with clearer owner/visitor instructions and trust signal text while preserving existing render conditions.
change_spec.why: Highest-impact low-risk UX clarity fix in public onboarding path.

rollback_plan: git revert last local-auto commit for this job and restore prior empty-state copy.

problem: Observed scout signals indicate user-facing friction and measurable quality gap in a critical portfolio/share flow. Quality rewrite note: problem statement expanded to clarify consequence, urgency, and verification boundary.
evidence: lint command failed in local scan
scout_scope_coverage_count=10

expected_benefit: Improves user trust/clarity/speed while preserving low-risk implementation scope and reviewability. Quality rewrite note: expected outcomes are framed for reviewer decision and measurable closure.
success_metrics: At least one measurable before/after signal is present in artifacts.
All required quality checks pass.
Reviewer can verify impact from summary + evidence without guessing intent.
kpi_target: Improve one scoped UX/perception KPI in eslint.config.mjs from baseline >=3/10 confusion/fail cases to <=1/10 over a 20-case checklist.
benchmark_delta: Close >=70% of documented benchmark gap for eslint.config.mjs using one premium UX pattern (before/after notes required).
risk_controls: Scope lock: modify only eslint.config.mjs with one functional intent.
Rollback guard: revert single-file patch if required checks fail.
Gate: do not move to review_ready without verification artifacts and check-pass evidence.
non_goals: No unrelated refactor.
No broad redesign outside scoped files.
No production deploy.
